services.name: StashDB
key_features: Comprehensive CRUD operations for all entity types
Strongly typed enums for categorical data

services.name: ThePornDB
key_features: Simplified schema focused on core queries

rate_limiting.best_practice: Use caching and implement exponential backoff

common_patterns: Both use GraphQL introspection for schema discovery
Both support scene matching via text search and fingerprints
Both use perceptual hash (PHASH) as primary video matching algorithm
Both provide performer/studio metadata alongside scenes
Both support user favorites for performers and studios
Both return scenes with nested performer/studio/tag relationships
best_practices: Always include duration with PHASH submissions for accuracy
Use GraphQL variables instead of string interpolation for security
Request only needed fields to minimize payload size
Implement caching for repeated queries (reduces API calls)
Use batch queries when searching multiple scenes
Handle rate limiting with exponential backoff
Validate PHASH distance (<8 for confident match)
Use consensus threshold (70%+) for ambiguous PHASH results
pitfalls: StashDB uses APIKey header, not standard Authorization Bearer
ThePornDB uses nested arrays for batch fingerprint results
Field names differ slightly (urls[].url vs urls[].view)
StashDB uses typed enums, ThePornDB uses strings
Some mutations may not appear in introspection (check documentation)
Rate limiting can cause unexpected failures without backoff
PHASH without duration can produce false positives
emerging_trends: GraphQL adoption for adult content metadata APIs
Perceptual hash as standard for video matching
Structured edit/draft workflows for data quality

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Implementing scene search in namer application
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use searchScene query with minimal field set, then fetch complete data for selected match
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Reduces payload size and API calls while maintaining good UX

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Calculate PHASH with duration, search both services, apply consensus logic (>70% threshold)
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Maximizes match accuracy and handles ambiguous results gracefully

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Implement token validation on startup, provide clear error messages for missing/invalid tokens
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Prevents runtime failures and improves developer experience

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use requests-cache with TTL, implement exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s...), batch queries where possible
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Reduces API load and handles rate limits gracefully

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Create provider abstraction layer that normalizes field names and data structures
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Allows seamless switching between services or parallel querying

popular_solutions: Community-driven hash submission for data quality
controversial_topics: Privacy concerns around hash submission and tracking
Data quality vs. ease of contribution
expert_opinions: PHASH provides excellent balance between accuracy and performance for video matching
GraphQL introspection enables self-documenting APIs and better tooling
Community submissions require consensus mechanisms to prevent bad data
